title: Production Script
description: Timestamped script produced by the Script Writer agent.

style_mode.description: Render mode locked at proposal. Carried into script so script-director can branch on tone/pacing without re-reading production_proposal.

target_duration_seconds.description: Aspirational duration target from the proposal. total_duration_seconds is the actual sum-of-sections; the two should agree within ±10%.

target_words.description: Word budget = round(target_duration_seconds * words_per_second). Optional convenience field.

total_words.description: Actual total word count across all sections — sum of sections[].word_count.

cta.description: Final call-to-action line (e.g. 'Start free at flowcut.io'). Optional — duplicates content from the cta_brand section but surfaced at root for downstream stages.

brand_name.description: Brand name as it appears in the cta_brand section. Optional — surfaced at root for asset-director / publish-director consumption.

beat.description: Optional ad-video beat label (e.g. 'hook', 'build', 'reveal', 'cta_brand'). Maps the section to its production_bible.narrative.emotional_beat_sequence position.

word_count.description: Word count of section.text. Sum across sections should equal script.total_words.

duration_estimate_seconds.description: Estimated section duration before TTS generation; should ≈ end_seconds - start_seconds. start_seconds + end_seconds remain canonical.

mode_annotations.description: Per-mode visual treatment cues for ad-video. Scene-director consumes these when expanding sections into scenes.

animated.description: What the animated treatment shows during this section.

cinematic.description: What the cinematic treatment shows during this section.

source_ref.description: Reference to the research_brief data point or URL that supports this claim. Every factual claim should be traceable.

tts_directive.description: Path A: per-section TTS pacing derived from the bible's beat intensity by lib.intensity_curve.derive_tts_directive. asset-director maps speed_mult to the active provider's speed/rate parameter (cosyvoice 'speed', etc.). Optional; absence = baseline 1.0 speed (legacy behavior).

speed_mult.description: Multiplier on the provider's nominal speech rate. Bounded to the cosyvoice safe range; most providers accept this.
